caption: "I've seen how addiction and wrong choices destroy lives. Now, I get to be part of their restoration instead of enforcement, helping individuals find healing, hope, and a new beginning." Steven Kaliaperumal, Centre Director at Teen Challenge Halfway House, brings both experience and heart to every resident's journey. As a former police officer, he understands the impact of addiction and poor choices, and now he walks alongside individuals as they rebuild their lives. Seeing lives transformed is what truly keeps him going.Under his leadership, the team continues to make Teen Challenge Halfway House a more welcoming space for healing. From creating warm communal areas and more green spaces to exploring new ways for residents to learn and work, these changes reflect the shared belief that dignity and hope should be part of rehabilitation.With leaders like Steven and a team committed to growing alongside their residents, no one walks this journey alone. Every step forward is a reminder that with guidance, care, and faith, second chances can become new beginnings.SPS SingaporePrisonService BeyondPrisonWalls SG4SecondChances

caption: "Why help? Why not."Meet Muzdalifah Binte Anuar or Efah, a mum of three, former SAF officer, Head of Home at a girls' orphanage, and a firm believer in second chances. Growing up, Efah didn't always walk the straight path. But along the way, people chose to see more than her mistakes and those second chances shaped the person she is today. Inspired by her father, a long-time prison religious counsellor, she now pays it forward by volunteering with the Singapore Prison Service, journeying with ex-offenders as they rebuild their lives.Despite a full schedule, volunteering grounds her. It's in the quiet conversations, the listening ear, the simple "thank you for coming" moments that remind her that every interaction is one filled with hope, regret, and love for their family."You don't need to be perfect. Just show up with a willing heart. Sometimes, believing in someone is the second chance they've been waiting for."Show her your support in the comments!SPS SingaporePrisonService BeyondPrisonWalls SG4SecondChances

caption: The Turning Point Halfway House, an all-women facility, provides a safe and caring space where residents can rebuild their lives through personalised support, counselling, and life-skills programmes. Christina Arumugam, Executive Director, is dedicated to guiding women through their journey of reintegration with compassion and purpose.For over 35 years, The Turning Point Halfway House has been supporting residents through challenges they face during reintegration, including mental health struggles and strained family ties.Christina and her team ensure guidance and care are always available when residents need them. In this nurturing space, quiet progress is celebrated, and second chances are always possible. SPS SingaporePrisonService BehindPrisonWalls SG4SecondChances

caption: By day, they are CaptainsOfLives, dedicated to the rehabilitation and transformation of others. And they make their rest time count by contributing to Singapore's SEA Games pursuit.We are incredibly proud to celebrate the historic achievement of our Captains of Lives at this year's SEA Games. Representing Singapore for the first time in Tug of War, the team, comprising officers from various agencies, dug deep to clinch a hard-fought bronze in the Mixed Team category. Finding time to practice while balancing shift work and family time, their journey to the podium was built on grit and an unbreakable spirit.Congratulations to RO Muhammad Irsham Samat, RO Ke Wai Choong, and SGT Noorlizawati Ramdan for showing us what it means to lead both in and out of uniform. We look forward to celebrating their future achievements as they continue to inspire on and off the field.SPS SingaporePrisonService SG4SecondChances SEAGames2025

caption: It was a special day at Institution TM1 for the National Youth Achievement Award (NYAA) Ceremony 2025! For 25 years, the NYAA programme has been a platform for young inmates to learn, grow, and serve.This year's theme, 'Connecting Hearts, Rebuilding Lives, Shaping Singapore Together', perfectly captures the spirit of our participants. We celebrated 58 inmates who showed dedication, resilience, and a willingness to give back. Among them, 46 received Gold, 10 Silver, and 2 Bronze Awards a huge congratulations to all! We were honoured to have Senior Parliamentary Secretary, Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth & Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment, Ms Goh Hanyan, as our Guest-of-Honour. She encouraged participants to continue inspiring others and acknowledged the dedicated partners who have supported NYAA over the past 25 years.We also heard from Mr Fairizal, an NYAA alumnus and desistor, who shared how the programme shaped his own journey. He reminded our participants that NYAA is more than an award. It is a chance to rediscover their strengths, build resilience and use what they have learnt to uplift those around them. His story is a powerful reminder that change is possible and that their journey is just beginning. SPS SingaporePrisonService BehindPrisonWalls SG4SecondChances NYAA2025

We were honoured to have Senior Parliamentary Secretary, Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth & Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment, Ms Goh Hanyan, as our Guest-of-Honour. She encouraged participants to continue inspiring others and acknowledged the dedicated partners who have supported NYAA over the past 25 years.We also heard from Mr Fairizal, an NYAA alumnus and desistor, who shared how the programme shaped his own journey. He reminded our participants that NYAA is more than an award. It is a chance to rediscover their strengths, build resilience and use what they have learnt to uplift those around them. His story is a powerful reminder that change is possible and that their journey is just beginning. SPS SingaporePrisonService BehindPrisonWalls SG4SecondChances NYAA2025

caption: For nearly four decades, Mr. Solomon Joseph has dedicated himself to creating genuine, positive change. As an SPS Prison Volunteer, he has spent countless hours walking alongside inmates, proving that transformation is always possible.This International Volunteers Day, we celebrate the powerful impact of all SPS Prison Volunteers in the rehabilitation and reintegration of inmates and ex-offenders.Solomon's story reminds us that volunteering is not just about giving; it is about being present when it matters most."Each time I step into prison, my hope is that the inmates will not go back to their old ways and embrace a new direction in life. I have seen many who, after their release, go on to live good and meaningful lives. While many of my peers choose to slow down in retirement, my journey is different I find great satisfaction in guiding inmates, touching their hearts, and witnessing their lives being transformed."Today, we honour all those who make hope possible. Keep a lookout as we showcase other inspiring prison volunteers in the coming months! SPS SingaporePrisonService BehindPrisonWalls SG4SecondChances InternationalVolunteersDay

On 20 November 2025, they graduated from the SPEAR Tactical Course and embarked on a new stage of their career. Supported by mentors and trainers at SPEAR, as well as their loved ones at home, we hope they will continue to excel in their new roles as SPEAR Operators. SPS SingaporePrisonService BehindPrisonWalls SG4SecondChances

caption: For the Community Leadership Initiative as part of their National Youth Achievement Award (NYAA) Gold programme, 18 women inmates from Institution A4 partnered with the SG Cares Volunteer Centre @ Punggol, operated by and Precious Active Ageing Centre @ Punggol Ripples, to organise an engaging activity for the elderly residents on 9 September 2005, where they played traditional games and had meaningful conversations. The Community Leadership Initiative provided the NYAA inmate participants with a chance to make positive contributions to society and develop their leadership skills.By empowering inmates with opportunities like this, we can instil a sense of social responsibility in them and pave the way for their rehabilitation. caption: On 8 November 2025, in conjunction with the Yellow Ribbon Community Project (YRCP)s 15th anniversary, the SPS and hosted the annual YRCP Awards & Appreciation Luncheon to recognise the dedication of grassroots volunteers in supporting families impacted by incarceration. Sixteen Long Service Awards (LSAs) were presented to grassroots divisions and another 188 LSAs were presented to volunteers at the event to recognise their support and participation in the YRCP.Guest-of-Honour Acting Minister for Transport and Senior Minister of State for Finance, .jeffrey, announced the launch of a new YRCP Portal to automate and integrate the various systems used by volunteers for their engagements with families. Co-developed by SPS and , the YRCP Portal serves as an integrated platform that consolidates all volunteer-facing digital services into a single, user-friendly interface where YRCP volunteers can access all relevant information, track and monitor engagements through customised dashboards and offer multiple communication channels to deliver effective and coordinated care to the families they serve. A heartfelt thank you, , and the grassroots divisions, and YRCP volunteers for your continued efforts in supporting the inmates families. SPS SingaporePrisonService BeyondPrisonWalls SG4SecondChance SANA YRCP

Recognising that reintegration is a journey, Safriadi and his team provide personalised guidance, collaborate with community partners and leverage peer support from mentors who have been through rehabilitation. He also supports residents through meaningful activities such as caring for cats at the Cat Adoption House, a space that nurtures empathy and responsibility. For Safriadi, the most rewarding part of his work is seeing residents grow, rediscover their strength and take steps towards a brighter future, reminding him why second chances matter. SPS SingaporePrisonService BehindPrisonWalls SG4SecondChances

caption: For more than 20 years, group work has been a cornerstone of rehabilitation in the Singapore Prison Service. These sessions create safe spaces for individuals to reflect, confront past mistakes, and explore new ways to build meaningful lives. The book "Group Work in Correctional Rehabilitation: Practitioners' Experiences in Singapore Prison Service" captures the journeys of both practitioners and desistors, showing how shared stories, empathy, and guidance can help individuals rediscover hope and transform their futures. Through structured programmes, activities and peer support, participants learn to challenge unhealthy thinking, manage emotions, and make positive choices. Many, like Idid, go on to give back, mentor others and contribute to their communities. This book is a tribute to the courage, resilience and commitment of everyone involved, and a reminder that rehabilitation is a living reality that continues to ripple beyond prison walls. SPS SingaporePrisonService BehindPrisonWalls SG4SecondChances

caption: "For every resident who succeeds, it's a success worth celebrating." Meet Ruuveen Balachanthar, a Social Worker with the HEB-Ashram Halfway House since 2013. His journey in social work began by chance, but he chose to stay because of the fulfilment he found in helping others rebuild their lives.Through counselling, skills training and enhancing family support, Ruuveen works with residents to set realistic goals, build coping mechanisms and strengthen trust with their loved ones. For him, every success story, no matter how small, brings joy to the resident, as well as their families. It is this sense of fulfilment that has kept him going for over a decade. caption: Learning and competing with the best! From 22 to 26 September, SPS proudly hosted the 7th Asian Prisons Lockdown Challenge (APLC) 2025! This years edition welcomed eight teams, including three local teams comprising our CaptainsOfLives. Joined by participants from Brunei, Hong Kong, Malaysia, Macao, and South Korea, the participants competed in a series of intensive tactical challenges that tested their physical endurance, mental agility, teamwork, and decision-making under pressure. The event challenges included:Team Obstacles Challenge Sharpshooter Challenge Operation Tech-Secure Challenge Rescue & Restore Challenge and the NEW Specialist Challenge, a first-ever female-only challenge Beyond the competition, participants also exchanged tactical knowledge through demonstrations and sharing sessions, reinforcing APLCs mission of strengthening regional networks and advancing correctional excellence. The APLC remains more than a competition it is a platform for collaboration, knowledge exchange, and capability-building that strengthens correctional systems across the region. Congratulations to Macao , Hong Kong and South Korea for clinching the top three spots in this years competition! AsianPrisonsLockdownChallenge APLC2025 SPS SingaporePrisonService

Joined by participants from Brunei, Hong Kong, Malaysia, Macao, and South Korea, the participants competed in a series of intensive tactical challenges that tested their physical endurance, mental agility, teamwork, and decision-making under pressure. The event challenges included:Team Obstacles Challenge Sharpshooter Challenge Operation Tech-Secure Challenge Rescue & Restore Challenge and the NEW Specialist Challenge, a first-ever female-only challenge Beyond the competition, participants also exchanged tactical knowledge through demonstrations and sharing sessions, reinforcing APLCs mission of strengthening regional networks and advancing correctional excellence. The APLC remains more than a competition it is a platform for collaboration, knowledge exchange, and capability-building that strengthens correctional systems across the region. Congratulations to Macao , Hong Kong and South Korea for clinching the top three spots in this years competition! AsianPrisonsLockdownChallenge APLC2025 SPS SingaporePrisonService

caption: 27 participants from 14 countries and jurisdictions arrived on 22 September 2025 for the 2nd International Correctional Leadership Programme (ICLP) in Singapore! Over the five-day programme, participants engaged in meaningful discussions on rehabilitation and reintegration, explore different approaches to corrections, and strengthen collaboration in correctional leadership.We also hosted the Asian Prisons Lockdown Challenge (APLC) 2025! Participants joined us at the opening dinner, marking the start of a meaningful week of collaboration and camaraderie. As host, SPS is honoured to provide this platform for global learning and partnership, and we look forward to the ideas and experiences that continue to inspire our collective journey towards safer communities and second chances. ICLP2025 SPS SingaporePrisonService BehindPrisonWalls SG4SecondChances

caption: Celebrating 25 years of transforming lives at the Yellow Ribbon CARE Network Gala Dinner 2025!The Gala was graced by Guest-of-Honour Minister for Law and Second Minister for Home Affairs Mr , alongside more than 800 community partners, donors, sponsors, beneficiaries, and representatives from the CARE Network agencies, to celebrate 25 years of transforming the lives of inmates, ex-offenders and their families.Guests enjoyed musical performances by inmates from the Performing Arts Centre, prison officers, and students from , .sg and took part in auction segments to raise funds. ComChest, the official charity partner for the event, helped facilitate the fundraising efforts. Through the event, over $1.7 million was raised for the Yellow Ribbon Fund, , and .Two new initiatives were also launched during the event:YR Cares, a new charity formed through the merger of the Industrial and Services Co-operative Society Limited (ISCOS), NeuGen Fund, and YRF. Lim Tze Peng-Yellow Ribbon Fund STAR Bursary, to recognise the contributions of the late Mr Lim Tze Peng, a Cultural Medallion recipient, who generously donated his artworks to raise funds for the Yellow Ribbon Fund (YRF).YellowRibbonSingapore YellowRibbonProject SingaporePrisonService SPS BeyondPrisonWalls YRCares SG4SecondChances BeyondSecondChances SANA NeuGen
